title: Proton Transparency and Neutrino Physics: New Methods and Modeling
authors: S. Dytman, M. Betancourt, N. Steinberg, L.B. Weinstein, A. Ashkenazi et al.
summary: New data-driven measurement of proton transparency using CLAS electron-nucleus scattering on He, C, and Fe targets. Transparency is defined as the ratio of events with a detected proton to quasi-elastic events where a proton should have been knocked out, avoiding model dependence of earlier extractions. Results are consistent with previous measurements but reveal that GENIE needs improved nuclear ground-state and FSI modeling, particularly at low proton momenta.
imageAlt: Proton transparency vs momentum for He, C, Fe

title: A universal implementation of radiative effects in neutrino event generators
authors: J. Tena Vidal, A. Ashkenazi, L.B. Weinstein, P.G. Blunden, S. Dytman, N. Steinberg
summary: Implements QED radiative corrections, real photon emission by incident and scattered electrons, plus virtual-photon exchange and vacuum polarization, into neutrino event generators using the extended peaking approximation. Validated within GENIE, the framework enables proper unfolding of radiative effects when benchmarking electron-nucleus data against generator predictions, closing a long-standing gap in e-A/nu-A comparisons.
imageAlt: Radiated vs unradiated eGENIE missing energy and momentum

title: Electron scattering and neutrino physics
summary: Comprehensive topical review on how electron-nucleus scattering data constrain neutrino interaction models across the QE, resonance, DIS, and MEC channels from tens of MeV to several GeV. Outlines the critical role of e-A measurements in reducing nuclear-interaction systematics for DUNE, Hyper-K, and the broader oscillation program. A community white-paper with 53 authors spanning the e-A and nu-A fields.
imageAlt: Kinematic coverage of e-A data on carbon overlaid with DUNE flux
citationText: J. Phys. G: Nucl. Part. Phys. 50 120501 (2023)

title: Electron-beam energy reconstruction for neutrino oscillation measurements
authors: M. Khachatryan, A. Papadopoulou, A. Ashkenazi et al.
summary: Landmark e4nu result exploiting the known electron beam energy as a proxy for the unknown neutrino energy to benchmark energy-reconstruction methods on CLAS data (C, Fe targets). Demonstrates that calorimetric reconstruction outperforms QE-based approaches, reducing biases from nuclear effects. Reveals that current neutrino generators mis-reconstruct the incident energy at the few-percent level, directly relevant for oscillation delta_CP measurements.
imageAlt: QE reconstructed energy showing data vs GENIE SuSAv2 and G2018

title: Inclusive Electron Scattering And The GENIE Neutrino Event Generator
authors: A. Papadopoulou, A. Ashkenazi et al.
summary: First systematic test of GENIE's electron-scattering mode against inclusive (e,e') cross-section data. Incorporates radiative corrections and the SuSAv2-based QE+MEC model into the generator. Shows adequate agreement at lower beam energies but exposes discrepancies in the RES/DIS regime at higher energies, charting the path for improved nuclear modeling in neutrino generators.
imageAlt: Inclusive C and Fe cross sections comparing GSuSAv2 and G2018 to data
